novel_features.name: Streak & gap tracker
novel_features.description: See your current and longest daily-shooting streaks and every missed day in a range.
novel_features.rationale: Only local SQL over ten years of capture times can compute day-level coverage in one pass.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when asked about daily practice consistency or missed days.

novel_features.name: Daily pick resolver
novel_features.description: Get exactly one image per day — flag beats rating beats recency — with its file path resolved for publishing.
novel_features.rationale: Encodes the photographer's selection ladder against the live catalog with no export step.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a build pipeline needs the day's photo, not a list of candidates.

novel_features.name: On-this-day
novel_features.description: Everything shot on this calendar date across all years, grouped by year.
novel_features.rationale: dateMonth/dateDay columns allow calendar-position queries no Lightroom feature offers.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for month/day browse pages and anniversary lookbacks; find only does date ranges.

novel_features.name: Project progress
novel_features.description: Progress report for a fixed-length photo project: day N of target, missed days, projected finish.
novel_features.rationale: Joins collection membership with capture dates for finite-project accounting Lightroom cannot express.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when tracking a bounded series backed by a collection.

novel_features.name: Shooting habits stats
novel_features.description: Histograms of your shooting by focal length, hour, weekday, month, camera, lens, or ISO.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates harvested EXIF across the whole catalog; Lightroom filters one facet and never counts.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for gear and habit questions; returns buckets, not image rows.

novel_features.name: Catalog doctor
novel_features.description: Health sweep: masters missing on disk, images without capture time, orphan keywords, empty collections.
novel_features.rationale: Only a process on the same disk can stat every resolved path while staying read-only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run before backups or migrations to find catalog rot.

novel_features.name: Keeper funnel
novel_features.description: Conversion rates from shot to picked to rated to developed to collected, optionally per year.
novel_features.rationale: Chains image, develop-settings, and collection joins into ratios nothing else surfaces.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for selectivity and culling-discipline questions.

novel_features.name: Unedited backlog
novel_features.description: Keepers you flagged or rated but never developed.
novel_features.rationale: The develop-adjustments predicate requires a join Lightroom smart collections cannot express.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to surface workflow debt before publishing deadlines.

narrative.headline: Your Lightroom Classic catalog, queryable from the terminal — streaks, daily picks, and shot lists with zero exports and zero writes.
narrative.value_prop: Reads the .lrcat SQLite catalog Lightroom already maintains, strictly read-only. Built for daily photo practices: pick-of-day resolves the one image a publish pipeline needs, streaks and project track consistency, and photos turns any criteria into a JSON shot list.
narrative.auth_narrative: No authentication. The CLI opens the catalog file in SQLite read-only immutable mode and refuses all writes. Point it at a different catalog with --catalog or LIGHTROOM_CLASSIC_CATALOG.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the catalog is found and readable before anything else
First real read: what is actually in this catalog
The daily-practice headline: streaks and missed days
One image per day, path resolved, ready for a build script
Criteria search as a JSON shot list

troubleshoots.fix: The CLI opens read-only/immutable so this is rare; if it appears, close Lightroom's backup dialog or pass --catalog pointing at a copied .lrcat
The path is not a Lightroom Classic catalog; check --catalog points at a .lrcat file, not .lrcat-data
No photos that day; run streaks to see gap days, or widen with --since/--until

narrative.when_to_use: Use for any question about a Lightroom Classic catalog: what was shot when, with what gear, which images are picked or rated, and where the files live on disk. Built for photographers running daily practices and for build scripts that need per-day shot lists as JSON.
anti_triggers: Do not use to edit photos, change ratings, or write anything into the catalog — it is strictly read-only
Do not use for Lightroom (cloud) or Apple Photos libraries; this reads .lrcat files only
Do not use to render or export image files; it resolves paths but never touches image pixels

recipes.title: Today's pick for the photo-a-day site
recipes.explanation: Returns the single best image for today with its absolute path, ready for a publish script.

recipes.title: Which days this month have no photos
recipes.explanation: Narrow the streak report to just the missing dates.

recipes.title: Shot list of recent keepers
recipes.explanation: Uses --select to keep the JSON small for agent consumption.

recipes.title: Gear reality check
recipes.explanation: Counts by lens with first/last-seen dates — what glass actually gets used.

recipes.title: Pre-backup health sweep
recipes.explanation: Finds masters missing on disk and other catalog rot before it bites.

trigger_phrases: pick of the day from Lightroom
lens usage stats from my catalog
